name: Tamales with Chicken and Salsa Verde
recipeInstructions:
PREPARE CORNHUSKS:
Simmer the cornhusks in water that covers them for 10 minutes. Use a weight to keep them submerged. Let stand until pliable.
TO MAKE THE DOUGH:
Beat lard in a mixer until light (about 1 minute). Add masa, beating well. Alternate adding more masa and broth until the batter is medium-thick. Sprinkle baking powder and salt, then beat again for 1 minute.
TO MAKE THE SALSA:
Husk and wash tomatillos. Boil with Serrano chiles and a pinch of salt until tender (10-15 minutes). Process in a food processor with coriander, onion, garlic, cilantro, and remaining tomatillo puree. Heat lard, then cook the sauce until dark and thick, stirring constantly for 45 minutes.
Mix shredded chicken with half the salsa.
TO FORM THE TAMALES:
Pat husks dry and cut into strips (1/4 inch wide, 7 inches long). Place a strip on a cornhusk square, spread dough mixture, fold sides over filling, and secure top. Repeat with remaining husks.
Arrange tamales in steamer layers, leaving space for expansion. Cover with leftover husks and steam for 1 hour, adding water as needed.
Serve with additional salsa.
Yield: 4-6 servings
